1.7: A direct revelation: 'For they have gone out for the sake of the name, accepting nothing from the Gentiles.' The missionaries who deserve hospitality go out 'for the sake of the Name' (hyper tou onomatos) — the Name of Jesus, YHWH's eschatological Name in the NT (Acts 4:12; Phil 2:9-10). They are supported not by the Gentile world's resources but by the community of believers. The missional church is defined by: (1) the Name as motivation, (2) non-worldly financial support, and (3) mutual community support (synergoi, v. 8). The entire brief letter is framed within this Christological missiology.
